title: Inconsistent naming: Record::parse() in lichen-senml vs from_bytes() everywhere else
description:
All codec types across the workspace use from_bytes() for parsing:
- CoapPacket::from_bytes()
- LichenFrame::from_bytes()
- Fragment::from_bytes()
- Ipv6Header::from_bytes()
- Dio::from_bytes(), Dao::from_bytes()
- Announce::from_bytes()
- BlockOption::from_bytes()

But lichen-senml/src/record.rs uses:
  pub fn parse(data: &'a [u8]) -> Result<Self, CborError>

What goes wrong: Inconsistent naming makes the API harder to discover and remember. Users familiar with from_bytes() across the codebase will not find parse() without documentation.
